threats.title: Weak Authentication Mechanism
threats.description: The system lacks details regarding its authentication mechanisms. Without knowing if multi-factor authentication (MFA) is implemented, or if password policies are enforced, attackers could potentially spoof user identities to gain unauthorized access. This could involve brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, or phishing campaigns targeting weak passwords.
threats.attack_vector: Exploitation of weak password policies, successful credential compromise through phishing or brute-force attacks, or leveraging vulnerabilities in the authentication protocol itself.
mitigations: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users.
Enforce strong password policies (minimum length, complexity, regular rotation).
Regularly audit user accounts and disable inactive accounts.

threats.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
threats.description: Due to the lack of information on input validation and sanitization, the system is potentially vulnerable to SQL injection attacks. Attackers could inject malicious SQL code into input fields to manipulate database queries, potentially leading to data breaches, data modification, or even complete system takeover.
threats.attack_vector: Injection of malicious SQL code through user input fields (e.g., search boxes, login forms, data entry forms) that are not properly sanitized or validated.
mitigations: Implement parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ input validation and sanitization to filter out malicious characters and code.
Regularly update database software to patch known vulnerabilities.

threats.title: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
threats.description: Without knowing the system's architecture or the types of operations performed, it’s difficult to assess the risk of a denial-of-service (DoS) attack.  If the application is not designed to handle a large number of concurrent requests or if it contains vulnerabilities that can be exploited to consume excessive resources, it could be susceptible to a DoS attack, leading to service unavailability.
threats.attack_vector: Exploitation of vulnerabilities in the application code that cause it to consume excessive resources (e.g., CPU, memory, network bandwidth), or a large-scale attack targeting the application's infrastructure.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single source.
Employ input validation and sanitization to prevent malicious code from consuming excessive resources.
Utilize a Content Delivery Network (CDN) to distribute traffic and mitigate the impact of DoS attacks.

threats.title: Insufficient Authorization Checks
threats.description: The absence of details about the authorization mechanisms makes it difficult to assess the risk of privilege escalation. If authorization checks are not properly implemented, an attacker could potentially gain elevated privileges within the system, allowing them to perform actions they are not authorized to perform, such as modifying system configurations or accessing restricted data.
threats.attack_vector: Exploitation of vulnerabilities in the application code that bypass authorization checks, or leveraging vulnerabilities in the operating system or database to gain elevated privileges.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization checks for all user actions.
Regularly review and update authorization rules to ensure they are effective.
Employ a secure coding practice to prevent vulnerabilities that could lead to privilege escalation.

threats.title: Lack of Data Classification and Access Controls
threats.description: Without knowing the data classification scheme or the implemented access controls, sensitive data could be exposed to unauthorized users. This includes both personally identifiable information (PII) and potentially confidential business data.  Attackers could exploit misconfigured access controls or vulnerabilities to gain access to sensitive information.
threats.attack_vector: Exploitation of misconfigured access controls, vulnerabilities in the application code that expose sensitive data, or unauthorized access through compromised user accounts.
mitigations: Implement a data classification scheme to identify and categorize sensitive data.
Enforce the principle of least privilege, granting users only the minimum necessary access rights.
Regularly audit access controls and user permissions.

gaps: Weak Authentication Mechanism - Insufficient implementation of multi-factor authentication.
Lack of Data Classification and Access Controls - Limited focus on data sensitivity levels and granular access control policies.
priorities: Critical - Spoofing & Tampering (SQL Injection) - Immediate remediation required due to high severity and potential for significant data breaches.
High - Denial of Service & Elevation of Privilege - Requires prompt attention to mitigate service disruptions and unauthorized access.

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Authentication Mechanism
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: SQL Injection Vulnerability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-400
    owasp: A05:2021
    threat_category: Denial of Service
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Insufficient Authorization Checks
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Lack of Data Classification and Access Controls
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
